cross_scene_arcs.title: The Interrogation to Heist Pipeline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: DOG systematically progresses from extracting intelligence through torture to planning and initiating a high-stakes heist, demonstrating a calculated escalation from information gathering to operational execution.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: DOG uses torture to extract the location of hidden drugs and cash from Gordon.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: DOG leads the crew in loading the van with stolen goods, transitioning into active execution of the heist.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full consequences of DOG's reliance on extreme coercion and whether the heist will succeed remain uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Loyalty and Rising Tension with Plank
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: DOG's relationship with Plank evolves from hierarchical control to mutual distrust, culminating in direct confrontation as operational failures and power struggles erode their alliance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: DOG asserts dominance over Plank by enforcing merit-based discipline.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: DOG assumes full command after Plank is incapacitated, signaling a breakdown in trust and a shift in leadership dynamics.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Plank will accept DOG's authority long-term or seek retaliation remains unclear.

cross_scene_arcs.title: DOG's Cycle of Control and Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: DOG ascends to peak authority during the heist, only to suffer a total downfall when captured, then reasserts himself through vengeance, cycling between dominance, vulnerability, and authoritarian resurgence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: DOG takes decisive control during the Sloanes confrontation, asserting authoritarian leadership.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: DOG reinstates a regime of fear and torture to root out betrayal, reclaiming dominance after his capture.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The sustainability of DOG's violent authority and whether new threats will emerge from within his crew remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unresolved Threat of the Fourth Person
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: A persistent tactical conflict centers on the unknown whereabouts of a fourth person, creating ongoing risk and driving DOG's aggressive interrogation and control measures.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: DOG probes for potential links back to Plank and assesses security risks, noting the possibility of an unaccounted individual.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: DOG confirms one person is missing and secures the stash location, but the individual remains unlocated.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fourth person's status and potential to expose or disrupt the operation remain a latent threat.

cross_scene_arcs.title: DOG vs. Big Chris: Power Reversal and Retribution
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: DOG gains control of the heist loot only to be violently overpowered by Big Chris, then retaliates by hijacking his car and taking hostages, marking a volatile back-and-forth struggle for dominance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: DOG secures the stolen cash and prepares to ambush Ed and Bacon's gang, now in full control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: DOG hijacks Big Chris's car and demands the money back, but loses advantage after a crash, leaving the power balance uncertain.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The final outcome of the conflict, recovery of the money, and DOG's ability to survive Big Chris's retaliation remain unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Ascent and Fall of Eddy's Criminal Ambition
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddy evolves from an impulsive participant in street cons to a central figure in a high-stakes criminal operation, driven by a desire to prove himself and gain status. His ambition leads him to take increasing risks, culminating in a massive gambling debt that threatens his life and those around him. In response, he pivots from desperation to leadership, orchestrating a violent heist to reclaim power and settle the debt. The arc traces his transformation from naive aspirant to dominant strategist, achieving short-term success but at great moral and relational cost.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eddy is drawn to the spectacle and risk of street hustling, seeking excitement and belonging.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eddy achieves financial and operational control after a successful heist, declaring the threat neutralized and rewards distributed.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term consequences of his actions, including law enforcement scrutiny and internal guilt, remain unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Loyalty: Eddy and the Crew
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddy's relationship with his crew shifts from one of mutual participation to fractured leadership and eventual abandonment. Initially asserting control through skepticism and precision, he later becomes a liability when his debt implicates the entire group. Though he regains authority by devising a plan and rallying the crew for a dangerous mission, his ultimate decision to flee during the crisis reveals a breakdown in loyalty. The arc highlights the tension between collective survival and individual self-preservation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eddy distinguishes himself as meticulous and independent, challenging Bacon's authority over money handling.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eddy abandons the group in a moment of crisis, prioritizing self-preservation over solidarity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether the crew can survive without Eddy, and if trust can ever be restored, remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Debt and Power: Eddy's Descent into Obligation and Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddy's conflict with Hatchet Harry begins as a high-stakes poker game but escalates into a life-threatening debt that endangers him and his friends. This external pressure triggers an internal shift—from victimhood to aggression—as Eddy transforms the crisis into a justification for violent retribution. The arc charts his journey from indebtedness and vulnerability to reclaiming agency through force, illustrating how personal survival becomes entangled with collective risk.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eddy is bluffing with a weak hand under extreme pressure, risking everything in a game he cannot afford to lose.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eddy seizes control of Dog's house, reversing the power dynamic and asserting dominance through force.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The cycle of retaliation remains open, as Dog vows revenge and the consequences of violence loom.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Legacy and Identity: Eddy's Connection to JD and the Boxing Underground
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Eddy's status shifts dramatically upon entering the boxing gym, where his identity as JD's son grants him access and recognition in a world otherwise closed to outsiders. This inherited status affords him privileges—like selective entry and acknowledgment by Hatchet—but also subjects him to scrutiny and veiled threats. The arc explores how lineage functions as both protection and burden, positioning Eddy as a figure of interest whose actions reflect on his father's legacy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eddy is separated from his friends and granted exclusive entry to the boxing club, signaling special status due to his name.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Eddy is formally recognized by Hatchet and engaged in high-stakes play, confirming his place in the underground world tied to his father.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: It remains unclear whether Eddy will honor, reject, or redefine his father's legacy within this criminal ecosystem.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Narrative Control vs. Reality: Eddy's Performance Under Interrogation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Bookended by Eddy's interrogation, this arc frames his entire journey as a constructed narrative where he seeks to control perception. From the outset, he positions himself as the author of his story, not a passive suspect. The arc interweaves moments of triumph, failure, and betrayal, all filtered through Eddy's need to maintain intellectual dominance. Yet the contrast between his composed narration and the chaotic reality—his breakdown, betrayal of friends, and ultimate flight—reveals a deep dissonance between self-image and truth.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Eddy establishes narrative dominance in the interrogation room, reframing suspicion as performance.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Skeptic to Committed Participant
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tom evolves from proposing cautious, slow-moving financial schemes to fully committing to a high-risk armed retaliation plan, marking a transformation in his willingness to engage in direct criminal action.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tom proposes a scam that would take weeks, reflecting a preference for low-risk, long-term solutions.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tom unequivocally commits to participating in an armed confrontation, signaling full alignment with the group's violent strategy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The group still lacks clarity on how to execute the plan safely, and Tom's shift from skepticism to commitment occurs without resolution of the underlying risks.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Guardian and Enforcer of Group Discipline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tom consistently positions himself as Eddy's protector and the group's moral compass, intervening to enforce discipline, ensure preparedness, and maintain operational control, especially in moments of crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tom encourages Eddy to rest before a critical event, showing concern for his well-being.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tom confirms the arrival of targets and enables the raid, having transitioned into a key operational role within the group.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Eddy's defiance and ambiguous loyalty undermine Tom's authority, leaving the effectiveness of his protective role uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of Criminal Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tom's journey traces his attempt to construct a credible criminal identity through weapon acquisition and strategic input, culminating in a psychological breakdown that reveals the fragility of that constructed self.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tom negotiates product terms with authority, asserting control in a low-stakes exchange.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tom hesitates while discarding the stolen shotguns, climbs over a bridge railing, and nearly abandons his criminal life—only to retreat back, symbolizing inner collapse.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tom cannot fully let go of the guns or his criminal identity, indicating unresolved internal conflict about who he truly is.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalation of Violence and Moral Complicity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tom progresses from emotional outbursts to active participation in violent acts and morally compromised decisions, marking a descent into deeper criminal involvement with increasing personal and ethical cost.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tom attacks Eddy in a fit of rage upon hearing of Harry's threat, showing emotional volatility.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tom delivers drugs to Nick, fully embedded in post-heist criminal logistics and complicit in covering up violent crimes.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term consequences of Tom's violent actions and moral compromises remain unaddressed, particularly regarding law enforcement or internal guilt.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Navigating the Shadow of Power
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tom moves from leveraging knowledge of hidden power structures to actively engaging with new, more dangerous figures like Rory Breaker, reflecting a growing immersion in the story's central criminal hierarchy and its associated risks.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tom narrates the power structure of Hatchet Harry's network, demonstrating awareness but distance from direct power.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tom helps justify a deal with Rory Breaker and later confronts total loss of resources, now fully entangled in the consequences of high-level criminal politics.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tom remains an outsider in understanding figures like Rory Breaker, and his analytical confidence may not protect him from real danger.
